lines: Yesterday's chaos delivered a 1–1 between Netherlands and Morocco, which means Al watched his Dutch lads bottle it while Butters, Callum, Jim, and Wilmot all punched the air simultaneously — four people sharing one dopamine hit like it's a timeshare in Benidorm. Paraguay also nicked a 1–1 against Germany, so Ellis is still alive, somehow, despite South Korea and New Zealand already being absolutely cooked.

Special mention to The Foreman, who is sitting 10th — LAST — with Türkiye and Curaçao already binned off, clinging to France and Colombia like a man on a liferaft made of other people's good luck. Mate, two of your four teams are already on holiday. The other two aren't even yours spiritually.

Today's big one: France vs Sweden. Callum, Croft, Dene, and The Foreman are all backing Les Bleus, while Jim — sitting joint 6th — is the lonely Sweden truther in the room. Jim, if Gyökeres or whoever Sweden are relying on puts one past Maignan, you absolute legend. If not, you're watching your last horse get shot.

Then there's the Norway match — Côte d'Ivoire vs Norway — and frankly half the sweepstake has a stake in this one. Al, Butters, Callum, Croft, and Wilmot all own a slice of Norway, meaning a single result could simultaneously delight and devastate multiple people on the leaderboard like some kind of footballing emotional landmine.

Watch out for Norway today — if they go through, Butters (joint top, 49pts) gets a boost across THREE of his teams (Portugal, Morocco, AND Norway still all in), which would be genuinely disgusting and he doesn't deserve it.
